title: calluna vulgaris subf. chlorostachys
intro: Calluna vulgaris subf. chlorostachys, also known as the heather or ling, is a subform of the Calluna vulgaris plant species, which is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to Europe, particularly in acidic soils. The medicinal properties of Calluna vulgaris subf. chlorostachys include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to help alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Calluna vulgaris subf. chlorostachys is typically prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
